columns.name: l_orderkey
columns.index_rationale: FK join key in Q3. Build hash table during scan for probe by orders. ~6M distinct values, hash table fits in L3 cache (24-48MB with load factor 0.75).
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality (6M distinct), no compression benefit.

columns.name: l_linenumber
columns.index_rationale: Part of PK but not used in queries.
columns.encoding_rationale: Small values (1-7 typical), use 1 byte instead of 4 to save space.

columns.name: l_quantity
columns.index_rationale: Range filter in Q6 (quantity < 24), but applied after date filter which is more selective. Zone maps provide sufficient pruning.
columns.encoding_rationale: Floating point values, compression difficult.

columns.name: l_extendedprice
columns.index_rationale: Used in computations, not filters or joins.
columns.encoding_rationale: High precision decimals, no compression.

columns.name: l_discount
columns.index_rationale: Range filter in Q6, but only ~20% selectivity after date filter.
columns.encoding_rationale: Continuous values, no compression benefit.

columns.name: l_returnflag
columns.index_rationale: Used for grouping (4-6 groups total), perfect hash aggregation uses array indexing, not hash table. No index needed.
columns.encoding_rationale: Extremely low cardinality (~3 distinct values: 'A', 'N', 'R'). Dictionary encode to 2-bit integers for 4x compression and faster comparisons. Perfect for SIMD aggregation.

columns.name: l_linestatus
columns.index_rationale: GROUP BY column, but uses perfect hash aggregation (array-based).
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (~3 values: 'O', 'F'). Dictionary encode to 2-bit integers. Combined with l_returnflag creates ~4-6 groups total.

columns.name: l_shipdate
columns.index_rationale: CRITICAL: All 3 queries filter on l_shipdate with high selectivity (14-98%). Sorted storage enables: (1) Binary search for range queries, (2) Zone maps for block pruning (skip 50-90% of blocks), (3) Sequential access after filter. Date filters are the PRIMARY selectivity driver - optimizing this column is the #1 priority for I/O reduction.
columns.encoding_rationale: Sorted dates enable delta encoding. Store first date + 16-bit deltas (dates span ~7 years = ~2500 days, fits in uint16_t). 2x compression (4 bytes → 2 bytes per value).

columns.name: l_commitdate
columns.encoding_rationale: Dates likely clustered with l_shipdate, delta encoding provides 2x compression.

columns.name: l_receiptdate
columns.encoding_rationale: Dates clustered with l_shipdate, delta encoding provides 2x compression.

columns.name: l_shipinstruct
columns.encoding_rationale: Likely low cardinality (limited set of shipping instructions). Dictionary encoding can achieve 10-50x compression if <100 unique values.

columns.name: l_shipmode
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (7 ship modes in TPC-H). Dictionary encoding achieves 10-50x compression.

columns.name: l_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality free-text, no compression.

lineitem.storage_notes: CRITICAL: Sort entire table by l_shipdate for zone map effectiveness and range scan performance. Columnar storage with 64K-row blocks. Store hot column group (l_shipdate, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_quantity) contiguously for cache locality. Build zone maps with 8K-row granularity (min/max/count per block) on l_shipdate - expect 750 blocks, zone map fits in L1 cache (12KB). Expected I/O reduction: 10-100x on Q1/Q6 via block skipping.

columns.name: o_orderkey
columns.index_rationale: PK and join key in Q3. Build hash table on orders (after date filter: ~690K rows) for probe by lineitem. Hash table size ~20-30MB, fits in L3 cache. Hash join is 10-50x faster than nested loop for this join cardinality.
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality PK.

columns.name: o_custkey
columns.index_rationale: FK join key in Q3 (orders → customer). After customer filter (c_mktsegment='BUILDING'), only ~30K customers remain. Build hash table on customer, probe with orders.o_custkey. Bloom filter on this column can skip 80% of orders during scan.
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality FK.

columns.name: o_orderstatus
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (3 values: 'O', 'F', 'P'). Dictionary encode to 2-bit integers.

columns.name: o_orderdate
columns.index_rationale: Date filter in Q3 (o_orderdate < '1995-03-15') is ~46% selective. Sorted storage enables binary search and zone map pruning. Since lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate and they have non-overlapping date ranges in Q3, sorting orders by o_orderdate improves join efficiency via merge-style processing.
columns.encoding_rationale: Sorted dates enable delta encoding. 2x compression.

columns.name: o_orderpriority
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (5 priority levels). Dictionary encoding.

columns.name: o_clerk
columns.encoding_rationale: Moderate cardinality (~1000 clerks), dictionary encoding useful if <10% of row count.

columns.name: o_shippriority
columns.index_rationale: GROUP BY column in Q3, but grouping on l_orderkey is primary. No index needed.
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (1 value: 0), could use RLE but not worth complexity.

columns.name: o_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality free-text.

orders.storage_notes: Sort by o_orderdate for filter performance and zone map effectiveness. Columnar storage with 32K-row blocks. Zone maps on o_orderdate (min/max per block). Expected pruning: 50% of blocks skipped in Q3.

columns.name: c_custkey
columns.index_rationale: PK and join key in Q3. After c_mktsegment filter, only ~30K customers remain (20% selectivity). Build small hash table (~1-2MB) on filtered customers for orders probe. This is the optimal build side (smaller relation).
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality PK.

columns.name: c_name
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality, unique names.

columns.name: c_address
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality.

columns.name: c_nationkey
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (25 nations), but small enough (1 byte) that compression not needed.

columns.name: c_phone
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality.

columns.name: c_mktsegment
columns.index_rationale: Highly selective filter in Q3 (c_mktsegment='BUILDING' is 20% selective). Hash index enables O(1) lookup to pre-filter customers before join. Alternative: bitmap index (5 bitmaps for 5 segments). With 150K customers, 5 bitmaps = 5 × 18.75KB = 94KB total, fits in L1 cache.
columns.encoding_rationale: Very low cardinality (5 market segments: BUILDING, AUTOMOBILE, MACHINERY, HOUSEHOLD, FURNITURE). Dictionary encode to 3-bit integers. 8x compression + faster equality comparisons.

columns.name: c_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality free-text.

customer.storage_notes: Columnar storage. Consider sorting by c_mktsegment for clustering (improves filter performance via zone maps/RLE). Hot columns: c_custkey, c_mktsegment. Small table (150K rows), entire hot column group fits in L2 cache.

columns.name: n_nationkey
columns.index_rationale: Not accessed in sample queries. Tiny table (25 rows), full scan is trivial.
columns.encoding_rationale: 25 values, 1 byte each = 25 bytes. Compression overhead > benefit.

columns.name: n_name
columns.encoding_rationale: 25 unique strings, tiny table.

nation.storage_notes: Tiny dimension table - entire table fits in L1 cache (<2KB). Row storage acceptable (no projection benefit). No optimization needed.

columns.name: r_regionkey
columns.index_rationale: Not accessed in sample queries. Tiny table (5 rows).

region.storage_notes: Tiny dimension table - entire table fits in L1 cache (<1KB). Row storage acceptable.

supplier.storage_notes: Not accessed in sample queries. Minimal optimization - simple columnar storage for potential future queries.

columns.name: p_mfgr
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (5 manufacturers), dictionary encoding useful.

columns.name: p_brand
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (25 brands), dictionary encoding.

columns.name: p_type
columns.encoding_rationale: Moderate cardinality (150 types), dictionary encoding useful.

columns.name: p_size
columns.encoding_rationale: Values 1-50, use 1 byte instead of 4.

columns.name: p_container
columns.encoding_rationale: Low cardinality (40 container types), dictionary encoding.

part.storage_notes: Not accessed in sample queries. Columnar storage with dictionary encoding on categorical columns for space savings.

columns.name: ps_availqty
columns.encoding_rationale: Values 1-10000, use 2 bytes instead of 4.

partsupp.storage_notes: Not accessed in sample queries. Minimal optimization - columnar storage only.

design_rationale:
This storage design is optimized for the TPC-H analytical workload with emphasis on execution time. Key design decisions:

1. **Columnar Storage for All Tables**: All queries project only a subset of columns (4-7 out of 16 for lineitem). Columnar layout reduces I/O by 50-75% and enables vectorized execution.

2. **Sorted Storage on Critical Date Columns**: lineitem sorted by l_shipdate (all 3 queries filter on this), orders sorted by o_orderdate (Q3 filters on this). Sorted storage enables: (a) Binary search for range queries, (b) Zone map effectiveness (tight min/max per block), (c) Delta encoding compression, (d) Sequential access patterns for prefetcher.

3. **Zone Maps on Date Columns**: l_shipdate and o_orderdate get zone maps with 8K-row granularity. Expected block skipping: 50-90% in Q1/Q6, 50% in Q3. Zone maps are the #1 I/O optimization - more important than indexes for scan-heavy workloads.

4. **Hash Indexes on Join Keys**: Hash indexes on o_orderkey (orders PK), c_custkey (customer PK), l_orderkey (lineitem FK). These enable O(1) hash join probes instead of O(n log n) nested loops. Critical for Q3 where 3-way join dominates execution time.

5. **Dictionary Encoding on Low-Cardinality Columns**: l_returnflag, l_linestatus (2-3 values each), c_mktsegment (5 values), o_orderstatus (3 values), and categorical columns in part table. Dictionary encoding provides 4-50x compression + faster comparisons (4-byte int vs string). Particularly important for l_returnflag/l_linestatus in Q1 aggregation.

6. **Delta Encoding on Sorted Dates**: l_shipdate, l_commitdate, l_receiptdate, o_orderdate. Delta encoding on sorted dates achieves 2x compression (4 bytes → 2 bytes) with negligible decompression cost.

7. **Optimized Integer Types**: Use int8_t for small values (n_nationkey, l_linenumber, p_size, c_nationkey), int16_t for medium values (ps_availqty). Reduces memory footprint and improves cache efficiency.

8. **Minimal Optimization for Cold Tables**: nation, region, supplier, part, partsupp not accessed in sample queries. Basic columnar storage only, no indexes. Dictionary encoding on categorical columns for space savings.

9. **Column Co-location**: Store frequently co-accessed columns contiguously (l_shipdate, l_extendedprice, l_discount always accessed together). Improves cache locality and reduces cache misses by 30-50%.

10. **Trade-offs**: Sorted storage on l_shipdate creates insertion overhead (O(n) inserts vs O(1) append). Acceptable for analytical workload (write-once, read-many). Hash indexes consume 20-50% extra space for load factor 0.5-0.8. Acceptable given 10-50x join speedup.

**Expected Performance Impact**:
- Q1: 10-20x speedup from zone maps (skip 98% of blocks), dictionary-encoded aggregation keys, vectorized execution
- Q6: 20-50x speedup from zone maps (skip 86% of blocks), sorted l_shipdate enables early termination, SIMD filters on compressed data
- Q3: 5-10x speedup from hash joins (3 hash indexes), filter pushdown (c_mktsegment dictionary lookup), date-based zone maps

**Memory Budget**: At SF=1, total data ~1GB uncompressed. With compression: ~400-600MB. Zone maps: ~50KB total. Hash indexes: ~100-150MB total. All hot data (lineitem + indexes) fits in typical L3 cache (32-64MB) for post-filter working sets.
summary: Columnar storage for all tables with sorted storage on l_shipdate and o_orderdate for zone map effectiveness. Hash indexes on join keys (o_orderkey, c_custkey, l_orderkey). Dictionary encoding on low-cardinality columns (returnflag, linestatus, mktsegment). Delta encoding on sorted dates. Zone maps on l_shipdate provide 10-100x I/O reduction via block skipping. Design optimized for Q3 (hash joins) and Q1/Q6 (zone map pruning + vectorized scans).
